title: Neuropsychologist
description: Neuropsychologists are specialists who assess, diagnose, and treat cognitive and behavioral disorders related to brain injury or neurological conditions. They use psychological tests and neuroimaging techniques to understand the relationship between the brain and behavior.
content: Neuropsychologists in India work in hospitals, rehabilitation centers, and private clinics. They evaluate patients with conditions like stroke, traumatic brain injury, dementia, and epilepsy. They provide therapy, cognitive rehabilitation, and counseling to patients and their families. This career requires strong analytical skills, empathy, and a deep understanding of neuroscience and psychology.

financialFeasibility.note: A master's or doctoral degree is required, which can be expensive. However, scholarships and educational loans are available. Earning potential increases with experience and specialization.

sectorScore.remarks: Opportunities exist in both government hospitals and private healthcare settings. Private practice is also a viable option after gaining sufficient experience.

relatedCareers.reason: Shares foundational skills in psychological assessment and therapy.
relatedCareers.title: Clinical Psychologist

relatedCareers.reason: Focuses on the diagnosis and treatment of neurological disorders.
relatedCareers.title: Neurologist
courses: MBBS - Bachelor of Medicine and Bachelor of Surgery

relatedCareers.reason: Deals with mental health conditions, often in collaboration with neuropsychologists.
relatedCareers.title: Psychiatrist

genderDistribution.note: The field is increasingly attracting more female professionals in India.
